title: verbascum glabrum
intro: Verbascum glabrum, also known as woolly mullein, is a biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Verbascum glabrum include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, with the flowers and leaves being used in various herbal preparations.
